scope_note: v0.4.0 declares this baseline as shared (one truth across languages). Per-agent quality is intended to compare apples-to-apples across runs regardless of target language. Decision D2 (resolved 2026-04-27 post-R2): Lenient default — one shared score across languages. Progressive fallback: if Phase B Python pilot shows ≥3pp quality_score divergence on any debt-bearer, that specific agent flips to per-language partition until its D6=Split rewrite ships. See docs/LANGUAGE-AGNOSTIC-DECISIONS.md and docs/R2-DEBT-REWRITE-FEASIBILITY.md.
note: Populated after first successful run (sdk-dragonfly-s2, 2026-04-18). Per-agent quality scores from metrics-collector (F1). Never lowered except via explicit learning-engine signoff (resets every 5 runs per settings.json baseline_reset_every_n_runs=5). Raise-only policy: improvements >10% over baseline trigger raise; regressions keep baseline and flag.

pipeline_level.computation_note: Mean of 4 primary agent quality scores (sdk-intake 0.985, sdk-design-lead 0.975, sdk-impl-lead 0.925, sdk-testing-lead 1.00). Weighted equally per metrics-collector F1. Previous run (sdk-dragonfly-s2): 0.95.

history.note: G90 first-attempt fail; recovered via out-of-band patch. Baseline 1.00 retained (no regression action; isolated Python-pilot-first-run G90 oversight).

sdk-design-lead.reference_notes: Raised from 0.85 (sdk-dragonfly-s2) to 0.975 (+12.5% > 10% raise threshold). Improvement driven by: Python pilot had 0 D3 review-fix iterations vs 1 in dragonfly-s2; Go G32/G33 guardrail-unavailability not applicable. Downstream impact 0.5 retained due to active-packages.json gap (surrogate-review pattern). D2-lenient-fairness: cross-language comparison deferred to next Go run.

history.note: Raised: +12.5% improvement. Go guardrail unavailability not applicable to Python run.

sdk-impl-lead.reference_notes: Baseline retained at 0.975 (sdk-dragonfly-s2). This-run score 0.925 (−5.1%). Regression flag recorded; G86 would fire at ≥5% delta but sample-size precondition (runs_tracked≥3) not met. Root cause: M10 rework wave (rework_score 0.5) triggered by asyncio.Lock+Condition throughput floor — hardware reality, not code quality regression. v1.1.0 TPRD drafted for throughput improvement. Next run comparison will clarify if this is a pattern or isolated.

history.note: Regression (-5.1%); baseline held at 0.975. G86 precondition not met (runs_tracked=2 < 3). Root: M10 rework on asyncio.Lock throughput floor.

sdk-testing-lead.reference_notes: This-run score 1.00 (+2.5% improvement). Below 10% raise threshold; baseline held at 0.975. Improvement factors: downstream impact improved from 0.5→1.0 (6 baselines correctly seeded vs dragonfly-s2 waiver at H8). Soak passed at exactly MMD+0.38s. CALIBRATION-WARN correctly not scored as failure.

history.note: Improvement +2.5%; below raise threshold. Baseline held at 0.975.

d2_cross_language.cross_language_delta_status: N/A-this-run — first Python run establishes Python seed; formal Go-vs-Python comparison deferred to next Go run
d2_cross_language.note: quality_score stored as 1.00 (formula score = perfect output per formula components). self_reported_score of 0.91 is the devil's own quality assessment of the design under review — not the devil's quality_score as an agent. The formula-based quality_score for the sdk-design-devil agent is 1.00.

raise_policy.description: Raise baseline only if current score exceeds baseline by >10%. Keep baseline if regression. Never lower without learning-engine reset authorization.
